requirementCueLines: General History Option | Department of Social Sciences | University of Washington Tacoma

The General History option is designed to offer students a survey of history and build upon the core requirements. In addition to the 30 credits of Core courses, this option requires 30 credits of Electives with a THIST prefix; 25 credits must be upper-division (300–400 level) THIST courses. For THIST options, please see the complete list of course offerings .
THIST 380 Humanities Research and Writing (taken in junior year—recommended prerequisite: THIST 101)
THIST 498 History Capstone (2.0 GPA minimum required; taken in your last 1–2 quarters; prerequisite: THIST 380 with a minimum 2.0 GPA)

ownerTitle: History (BA) - Arts, Culture and Society option
primarySourceLabel: UW Tacoma History Arts, Culture and Society option requirements
sourceLabel: UW Tacoma History Arts, Culture and Society option requirements
requirementCueLines: The Arts, Culture and Society option is designed to offer students a strong foundation for understanding the interconnection between cultural production and historical causation where ideas, art, architecture, literature, film and the performing arts function as agents of social and historical change. This option examines the intersection and interaction between politics, science, economics, social ritual and development, class, gender and race across a global environment over time.
THIST 380 Humanities Research and Writing (taken in junior year—recommended prerequisite: THIST 101)
THIST 498 History Capstone (2.0 grade minimum required; taken in your last 1–2 quarters; prerequisite: THIST 380 with a minimum 2.0 grade)
30 credits from the list below; 25 credits must be upper-division (300–400) level THIST courses.
TARTS 360 Women Artists from the Renaissance to the Present VLPA
TARTS 480 Contemporary Art and Society Since 1945
TEGL 419 African American Culture and Consciousness
THIST 290 A World History of Food

primarySourceLabel: UW Tacoma Global History option requirements
sourceLabel: UW Tacoma Global History option requirements
requirementCueLines: The Global History option is designed to offer students a strong foundation for understanding the relational forces between continents and the historical process of globalization. Colonialism, imperialism, anticolonial independence movements and the national and transnational effects they cause are additional areas of study. This option prepares students for investigating issues of globalization, such as the impact of colonization and aggressive imperial expansion on dominated territories and their history.
THIST 380 Humanities Research and Writing (taken in junior year—recommended prerequisite: THIST 101)
THIST 498 History Capstone (2.0 GPA minimum required; taken in your last 1–2 quarters; prerequisite: THIST 380 with a minimum 2.0 GPA)
30 credits from the list below; 25 credits must be upper-division (300–400) level THIST courses.
TEGL 303 Introduction to American Indian Education
TEGL 340 Intersections: Race, Gender and Sexuality in a Global Perspective
THIST 260 Empires and Imperialism in World History
THIST 487 Technology in the Modern World
THIST 491 Advanced Topics in the Ancient and Medieval World

ownerTitle: History (BA) - Labor and Social Movements option
primarySourceLabel: UW Tacoma Labor and Social Movements option requirements
sourceLabel: UW Tacoma Labor and Social Movements option requirements
requirementCueLines: The Labor and Social Movements option is designed to offer students a strong foundation for understanding historical roots and processes that shape political, intellectual, economic and social developments and consequently the conditions of the working class in a global context. This option examines the culture, politics and socioeconomic conditions as they intersect with gender, labor and race in changing contexts of im(migration), famine, disenfranchisement, marginalization, oppression and political disempowerment. Consequently, this option explores and analyzes social movements responding to these conditions such as socialism, protest, community organization, unionism and revolution.
THIST 380 Humanities Research and Writing (taken in junior year—recommended prerequisite: THIST 101)
THIST 498 History Capstone (2.0 GPA minimum required; taken in your last 1–2 quarters; prerequisite: THIST 380 with a minimum 2.0 GPA)
30 credits from the list below; 25 credits must be upper-division (300–400) level THIST courses
THIST 322 American Labor Since the Civil War
THIST 336 Black, Labor and Protest Music in Historical Perspective
THIST 365 Europe in the Twentieth Century
THIST 376 Global Diasporas, Borders and Refugees in Visual Culture
THIST 416 Life and Thought: Martin Luther King, Malcolm X and Angela Davis
THIST 441 Black Freedom Movement in Perspective
TPOLS 343 Community and Labor Organizing: A Multicultural Perspective

ownerTitle: History (BA) - Power, Gender and Identity option
primarySourceLabel: UW Tacoma Power, Gender and Identity option requirements
sourceLabel: UW Tacoma Power, Gender and Identity option requirements
requirementCueLines: The Power, Gender and Identity option offers a strong foundation for understanding the historical roots of intersections between race, gender, ethnicity, class and socioeconomics that have created and continue to transform hierarchical structures of power. This option examines the origins of social stratification with regard to race, gender, ethnicity and class. In consultation with primary sources drawn from divergent cultural, social and natural science documents, this option explores the historical context of marginalization, disenfranchisement, political and economic inequality and disempowerment.
THIST 380 Humanities Research and Writing (taken in junior year—recommended prerequisite: THIST 101)
THIST 498 History Capstone (2.0 GPA minimum required; taken in your last 1–2 quarters; prerequisite: THIST 380 with a minimum 2.0 GPA)
30 credits from the list below; 25 credits must be upper-division (300–400) level THIST courses.
TEGL 303 Introduction to American Indian Education
TEGL 340 Intersections: Race, Gender and Sexuality in a Global Perspective
TEGL 380 Gender and Sexuality across Cultural and Historical Contexts
TEGL 419 African American Culture and Consciousness
TLIT 433 Native American Literature and Federal Indian Law
TPOLS 317 The Politics of Race and Ethnicity in the United States
TSOC 265 Race and Ethnicity in the United States
TSOC 270 Introduction to Asian America: Sociological and Interdisciplinary Perspectives
TWOMN 347 History of Women in the United States
